parts.answer: the city and people in paragraph 12 The general effect is that people have been reduced to mere shadows of themselves, approaching a weird form of dried-up death. torpid: having or showing very little energy or movement, unsettling, eerie as if its unlubricated gears and belts had seized up: (image) nothing is moving, like an engine that is not oiled and has come to a stop, parts fused and useless, not functioning (people seemed to be) crumbling, turning to dust and salt : (image) disintegrating, breaking into pieces, weak, fragile a ghostly, powdery rabble: (image) almost transparent, barely human/alive, dried; disordered group, unnatural and unpredictable a strong wind could scatter them into clouds of dead skin: (image) exaggerated suggestion that they are so lifeless they would blow away in the wind, frail desiccated, dehydrated adults: thoroughly dried out, all moisture removed, dull and lifeless too tired to toil or trade: no energy for physical work/struggle or for usual business sluggish: lacking alertness, vigour, or energy, inert, unnatural for children

Aljafar, the cellar and its contents in paragraph 30 The general effect is to illustrate the villainous greed of Aljafar and convey the value and beauty of water. fumbling theatrically: (image) pretending to be struggling with the catch, making noise for effect, ridiculous and unconvincing attempt to deceive prised open: forced open, as if with a crowbar shoving: roughly pushing him to one side, not concerned about level of force used, dismissive of him, focused on water cavernous cellar: huge space opening up over and around them, treasure trove stocked ceiling-high with precious casks: have been ordered and organised to get as many in as possible, valuable resource, abundance of supply, reminiscent of (pirate) treasure releasing a translucent torrent: (image) gushing water, light passes through it but water is not transparent, pouring out with force suggesting large volume of water in the cask, freshness, contrasts with darkness of cellar crystal-clear water: valuable, as if precious gem, pure attached ourselves to lips-first like ticks to a sheep: (image) drinking greedily to quench their thirst, as if parasite sucking blood, desperate, disgusting image, dehumanising, fighting for survival hoarding: has been hiding it, keeping it privately and secretly for his own use, suggests greed May/June 2019 Marking Criteria for Question 2 Table A, Reading: Language analysis: Use the following table to give a mark out of 10 for Reading. Band 6: 9–10 • Wide ranging discussion of judiciously selected language with some high quality comments that add meaning and associations to words/phrases in both parts of the question, and demonstrate the writer's reasons for using them. • Tackles imagery with some precision and imagination. • There is clear evidence that the candidate understands how language works. Band 5: 7–8 • Explanations are given of carefully selected words and phrases. • Explanations of meanings within the context of the passage are secure and effects are identified
